scenarios.title: Delta — add waitlists to the course platform

top_gaps: Feedback type (bug/idea/question) and the feedback description have nowhere to live in the data model, yet UC-001 requires recording both.
status_history records no actor — contradicts the glossary definition and UC-003 AC-2 ('record who changed it').
members have no email or name, so UC-008 (invite by email) and AC-3 (reject duplicate email) are unimplementable as written.
status_history immutability is asserted by a live test but enforced by nothing in the model (no CHECK/trigger).
member_role contains an undocumented 'member' value; whether it is a valid assignable role is unclear for UC-009 validation.
Location/service-area matching is the core premise but unmodeled — providers have no zones, service_area is unused free text, and UC-001/003 location filtering cannot be built or tested
payments.amount is required with no derivation rule (hourly_rate × estimated_duration_hours is implied but never stated)
Refund contradiction: brief puts refunds out of scope, but UC-012 AC-2 / FL-006 mandate refund initiation, and payment_status has no 'refunded' value
UC-008 'return the booking request to the available pool' on payment failure is undefined given the unique booking↔request constraint
In-app provider notification is in scope but has no entity, mechanism, or task

Seat-tracking mechanism: nothing links an enrollment INSERT to courses.available_seats, yet UC-005/DA-2 expects a CHECK violation when a course is full — the decrement-on-enroll trigger (or app logic) is entirely unspecified, so the live test cannot pass as written.
Seat lifecycle on drop/complete: UC-005 recounts only on enroll while dropped rows are kept — the agent must guess whether dropping or completing frees a seat back.
Interface and access control: T-002 assumes an API with no contract, and Student-facing UC-002 ('view their own enrollments') has no student identity/authentication model to scope ownership.
No organization-creation / first-Owner bootstrap path — the tenant boundary and Owner role are defined but never instantiated by any use case, flow, or task.
Feedback submission needs a not-null end_user_id but the spec never states how the end_user is resolved from the submitted email (find-or-create vs reject).
No read/list use cases for the Inbox itself (listing/filtering feedback, viewing status history, listing comments/releases) even though the Inbox is the product's core concept.
Per-role authorization enforcement is only implied by per-UC actor labels; no AC rejects an out-of-role actor, and Owner-only 'destructive actions' are never enumerated.
No interface/transport layer specified — the agent must choose how the use cases are exposed (REST/GraphQL/RPC).
Authorization & ownership enforcement is assumed by the use-case narratives but unspecified and out of scope — who may accept/decline/complete/rate is undecided.
payment_status progression is ambiguous: ordered (pending→paid→refunded) per the brief vs. free enum per the model/use cases (no transition guard).
Status-transition triggers and a few data-integrity guards (window_end>window_start, star-range live test) are described but left for the builder to realize.
Capacity is modeled (courses.capacity) but never enforced — no UC/AC/DA rejects enrolling into a full course, leaving the brief's core anti-double-booking goal unimplemented.

Accepting a waitlist offer is said to 'convert to enrollment' (FL-005/UC-007), but no acceptance criterion or data assertion creates the enrollment row; its interaction with unique(student_id,course_id) and prior dropped enrollments is undefined.
Offer-next-seat automation is ambiguous: UC-006 lists Actor=Registrar but Trigger=Enrollment dropped — automatic policy or manual action?
The 'Course seats recounted' event (FL-001) has no backing entity, field, or use case.
Authentication: members sign in but there is no credential model or sign-in flow — the agent must choose one to ship.

Cross-org integrity for assignment and release-linking is claimed 'structurally impossible' but the FKs don't constrain organization and no assertion tests a cross-tenant link — it's actually unspecified app-layer logic.
Status-transition validity graph is undefined (which lifecycle transitions are legal; whether 'declined' is reachable from any state).
Whether submitting a feedback item creates an initial NULL→new status_changes row, and who authors it given the submitter is a non-member end user while member_id is NOT NULL.
Role-based authorization (owner/admin/agent capabilities) is described in prose but has no acceptance criteria and no plan task, so wrong-role rejection is untested.
No capacity enforcement: nothing defines how a course is 'full' or makes enrollment reject/route to the waitlist when at capacity — yet the entire waitlist feature is premised on full courses.
FL-001's 'Recount course seats' policy and 'Course seats recounted' event have no use case, table column, task, or data assertion — the agent must invent what they compute and where the result lives.

The unique (student_id, course_id) index on enrollments conflicts with keeping dropped rows: re-enrolling or accepting a waitlist offer after a prior dropped enrollment in the same course would violate it — needs a partial-index decision.
